meta.seededNote: Seeded 2026-07-23 from the docs above. A working file (docs/roadmap/master-build-status.json) was used to assemble this and was retired in 476b7e8 so only one record exists; it is not listed here because a provenance path a reader cannot open is not provenance. Every item carries its own provenance to a doc that still exists.

mission.northStar: Kuma — your AI Software Healing Engineer.
mission.statement: Six specialist agents review every pull request, verify each finding against the real diff, and propose fixes a human approves. The long-term shape is a system that notices, diagnoses and heals software the way a living organism does. The review wedge is live; the platform ambitions are deliberately deferred, and recorded here with a reason so deferral stays a decision rather than a drift.

principles.title: Honesty is the product
principles.body: Never fabricate data, status or a passing result. Empty states say what is actually true, null (unavailable) is never [] (none), and a control that cannot act is disabled with its reason rather than a live-looking button.

principles.title: The human-in-the-loop moat
principles.body: Nothing merges or posts autonomously. The fix driver never advances past ready; only a human approves, only a human sends. A refusal branch nothing can trigger is dead code pretending to be a safeguard.

principles.title: Tenancy is a security boundary
principles.body: Every query is scoped by installationId, connection state is derived from getAccountState rather than re-derived locally, and a security gate has exactly one implementation - two copies are two places to drift, and drift here is a tenant leak.

principles.title: Self-telemetry is gated on a database fact
principles.body: Access is decided by isPlatformInstallation before any query runs, and an access denial is a distinct state from a backend outage. superlog.project_id is a self-dogfooding tag, not tenant isolation.

principles.title: Bring your own model
principles.body: Every tenant action rides the tenant's own key. Keys are encrypted at rest, never logged, never returned by an API, and never destroyed as a side effect - providers do not re-issue them.

principles.title: prisma migrate deploy, never db push
principles.body: All worktrees share one Postgres. db push syncs it down to your schema and silently drops other people's columns; it has done so three times.

principles.title: Separate moving from fixing
principles.body: Characterization tests come first on any untested surface. A refactor commit changes no behaviour and a fix commit moves no code; mixing them makes both unreviewable.

principles.title: Verify by driving the real flow
principles.body: A green suite is not a driven flow. Paste evidence. If you cannot verify something, say so - and never issue an unscoped DELETE against tenant data.

programmes.label: Reachability & Consolidation
programmes.caveat: The live sequencing document. Stage 1 is largely shipped; Stages 2-5 are unstarted. Its own text records that three of its items turned out to be builds rather than the wiring it predicted.

phases.label: Stage 1 - make the built product reachable
phases.evidence: 1192d37 (1.1, 1.2); 1.3 and 1.4 shipped; 1.5 blocked on Sentry's approval

phases.label: Stage 2 - Agents become a layer inside Services
Stage 4 - hygiene that keeps the map honest

programmes.label: SUPERLOG observability
programmes.caveat: Phases 0, 1, 2 and 4 closed with gate reports. Phase 2b was split out of Phase 2 and its first item is a live security gap, not an enhancement. Phase 3 is deliberately deferred.

phases.label: Phase 4 - trustworthy CI signal + harness hardening

programmes.label: Product roadmap (P1-P4)
programmes.caveat: STALE - this numbering lists work as unstarted that is closed in code (the approval-exec consumer, memory write-back and the review-heavy queue consumer were all recorded open after shipping). Correct it before planning off any number in this row.

phases.label: P3 - public API and the MCP inversion

programmes.label: Work-floor orchestration (A-C)
programmes.caveat: Phase A shipped as packages/orchestration. Phase B is blocked on the Docker sandbox workspace agent - the one plan artifact of ~380 declared symbols that was never written.

phases.label: Phase B - repro / root-cause / fix-author / test-author / QA agents

items.title: Agents
items.works: Per-agent chat is genuinely live against the connected model, with provider errors surfaced honestly. Findings come from real reviews.
items.gap: Every configuration control is local-only and never saved, and the page is scheduled for absorption into Services.

items.title: AI model connections
items.works: Connect, reconnect, set-active, disconnect and remove key. Ollama auto-detect with streamed model pull. Real diagnostics on failure. Re-connecting without re-entering a key preserves the stored key.

items.title: Data connectors
items.works: GitHub App install, Stripe API key, and PostHog + Vercel OAuth.
items.gap: Every other connector is disabled pending its integration being published.
items.evidence: app/(dashboard)/connections/[id]/page.tsx — non-published connectors still render disabled

items.title: Incident detail
items.works: Mark and unmark noise, view a linked fix run, scrubbed alert payload tables — and opening an investigation by hand now dispatches a fix container, the same route a critical alert takes.
items.gap: The fix it starts still halts at the human approve-and-send gate. The row previously said a hand-opened investigation could only be marked noise; that is no longer true.

items.title: Incidents list
items.works: Open / Resolved / Noise / All tabs over real incidents. New investigation creates a real Incident row.

items.title: Overview
items.works: Stat tiles, Sensorium code map, setup checklist derived from real DB facts, preset tabs, time range.
items.gap: Two checklist sub-steps (the coding-agent setup prompt, MCP Kuma) are honest 'coming soon' placeholders with nothing behind them. Planned telemetry connectors are inert.
items.evidence: packages/dashboard/src/lib/overview-setup.ts:12,80,104-105 — both steps are declared not-yet-shipped and render as honest placeholders

items.title: Review history
items.works: Server-filtered risk tabs, real review rows, pagination, honest empty state.

items.title: Search & notifications
items.gap: Command palette and notifications are both disabled placeholders. The breadcrumb is the only working part of the top bar.

items.title: Services
items.works: Repo rail, live synthesizer console over SSE, status board, Scan / Fix it / Dismiss against real endpoints, and both HITL gates (approve, then post the PR) now render on the work-item panel.
items.gap: Scan completion is a blind timer, and Fix/Dismiss still trigger a full-page reload that loses rail position. A fix exists on stroland02/overview-revamp (e0a3f65) but is NOT merged to main, so the behaviour is unchanged here.

items.title: Settings & billing
items.works: GitHub OAuth connect, a real Connections summary, real billing usage against the free-tier limit.
items.gap: No self-serve upgrade — stated plainly rather than faked.
items.evidence: packages/dashboard/src/app/(dashboard)/settings/page.tsx:266 states the missing self-serve upgrade outright rather than faking a checkout

items.title: Collector credentials come from the environment, not the committed config
items.works: otel-collector-config.yaml carries no credential literal — database/username/password resolve from CLICKHOUSE_DB/USER/PASSWORD. docker-compose supplies throwaway local defaults so `docker compose up` still works with no .env, and .env.example documents them. Verified by running the same validate the CI step runs.

items.title: Internal service-to-service auth is a short-lived signed token with a rotatable keyset
items.works: The static shared secret is retired. Callers present a short-lived HS256 JWT minted by @arete/internal-token with claims {iss, aud: arete-internal, iat, exp} and a kid header; the default TTL is 120s. Verification rejects missing, malformed, unknown-kid, bad-signature, expired and wrong-audience tokens with an undifferentiated 401, and answers 503 when the keyset is unconfigured rather than running open. The keyset maps kid -> secret with a separate activeKid, so a rotation can overlap (old kids keep verifying while new tokens mint with the new one) and dropping a kid revokes everything signed with it.

items.title: MCP OAuth: exchange is real, but the authorization request is not
items.works: The fabricated simulated_token_for_<code> is gone: _complete_auth performs a real code exchange, fails closed when token_url is unset, and stores nothing without a genuine access_token. Config is encrypted at rest and created atomically at 0o600. The OAuth state is now secrets.token_urlsafe(32), fresh per flow, and verified with compare_digest on BOTH the local callback and the manual-paste fallback — an unverified code is discarded before it is ever stored.
items.gap: Remaining, and smaller than it was: client_id is still hardcoded to "arete-client", the authorization endpoint is still guessed as the server target rather than discovered, and encryption no-ops to cleartext when no key is configured.

items.title: Prose-shaped credentials still reach sinks
items.works: Shaped secrets (sk-*, gh?_*, Bearer, key-ish URL params) and blocklisted object keys are scrubbed; URL query stripping now fires on embedded URLs anywhere in a string.
items.gap: A memory body containing a free-text credential matches no shape and no object key, and has no URL to strip. Verified stored verbatim. Fixing it means amending the frozen §5 pattern set, with real false-positive risk on ordinary prose — a decision, not a patch. Applies to every sink equally.
items.evidence: packages/telemetry/src/redaction.ts:26 — six shape patterns, none matches a bare prose credential

items.title: SecurityAssessor refuses instead of fabricating verdicts
items.works: assess_skill returns {status: "unavailable"} for every skill and carries no rating vocabulary, so no caller can render it as a score. The filename-substring Gen/Socket/Snyk verdicts are gone.

items.title: SSE endpoint is authenticated and tenant-safe
items.works: The route is guarded by requireInternalToken and sets no wildcard CORS header — it is consumed server-side or through an authenticated proxy.

items.title: Agent memory has no surface
items.works: Kuma really does record what it learns per repository and injects it into later reviews.
items.gap: Nothing displays it. Belongs on repository settings as 'What Kuma has learned', with an archive action.
items.evidence: packages/webhook/src/memory-write.ts — write path real; zero dashboard references

items.title: Approvals panel — the safety gate is visible, and rejectable
items.works: Session-scoped approve proxy plus a genuinely new reject route. 'No' was not an answer a human could give before — executeApproval's REJECTED branch was dead code. Tenancy proven live: a prompt under a different installation did not appear.

items.title: Approve a proposed fix
items.works: The gate renders on the Services work-item panel, reading the container's stored state. No /agents URL was created, so this is a step toward the Stage 2 absorption rather than against it.
items.evidence: components/dashboard/services/work-item-panel.tsx — 4 refs to the two gate buttons

items.title: Connect Sentry
blockedBy: ext:blocked on Sentry's own integration approval, not on us
items.works: The connector and its tests are fully built. Flipping connector-catalog.ts status:'planned' is the whole change.
items.gap: Blocked on Sentry's own integration approval, not on us.

items.title: Live throughput metrics — the dark wire
items.works: The worker publishes review-lifecycle metrics to a server-sent stream.
items.gap: No consumer — the source comments call it a dark wire. An Overview throughput tile needs a server-side proxy, which depends on the internal-token lifecycle decision.

items.title: Post the approved PR
items.works: Same panel as the approve gate; the dead !realMode branch is bypassed rather than revived.

items.title: Silence / un-silence a finding
items.works: POST /api/findings/[id]/noise is the dashboard's only noiseState writer, rendered by FindingNoiseControl on review detail. A human may assert only OPEN and SILENCED; UNDER_OBSERVATION and ESCALATED are machine-derived and render read-only, because a button asserting one would claim an observation that never happened.

items.title: Agent config changes are never saved
items.gap: Every parameter control in the config drawer is local-only and explicitly 'deliberately NOT persisted'. Without this, the Stage 2 agents layer ships parameter controls that are theatre — so either build it, or give them an explicit disabled state.

items.title: Agents layer inside Services
items.works: The locked decision, refined by the user 2026-07-22: agents are an additional layer within Services showing how they interact with the workflow; select the agents that are working to open their conversation and their parameter controls.
items.gap: Not started. Spec §0/§1 is locked; the wider Investigations slice around it is not greenlit.

items.title: Retire /agents as a nav destination
items.gap: Keep the route for deep-links; remove the tab once the absorption lands.
items.evidence: components/dashboard/sidebar.tsx still lists /agents as a nav destination

items.title: Decide the fate of the Agents-rail work inbox (built backwards)
items.gap: 73e2040 put the Services work inbox INTO the Agents rail — the reverse of the locked decision. It entrenches two pages instead of consolidating to one, and duplicates a section onto a page scheduled for absorption. Small, additive, tested and harmless while /agents exists, but it points the wrong way. Revert it, or let the absorption subsume it — not yet decided.

items.title: Back-to-Overview and sample-data links point where they say
items.works: Both "Back to Overview" links in reviews/[id] now go to /overview. The stale "Explore with sample data" copy was already removed.

items.title: Delete synth-ledger.tsx (zero importers)
items.gap: Still to do. The file exists at components/dashboard/agents/synthesizer/synth-ledger.tsx and no file under packages/dashboard/src imports it. An earlier pass on this lane checked components/dashboard/synth-ledger.tsx, the wrong path, and wrongly reported it deleted.
items.evidence: origin/main: packages/dashboard/src/components/dashboard/agents/synthesizer/synth-ledger.tsx exists; zero importers across src

items.title: Fix / Dismiss stops full-page reloading
items.gap: window.location.reload() is the only feedback, and it loses rail expansion, selection and stream position.

items.title: Kuma logo becomes a true global refresh
items.works: The logo already reflects real global loading state.
items.gap: Clicking it should re-pull services, connections, telemetry and scan state, turning the existing spin into honest progress. It must show real completion per source, and a source that fails must say so — a spin that resolves on a timer would be exactly the fabricated status the honesty rules forbid.

items.title: Manual investigations can start a fix
items.gap: The alert path (receiver.ts to routeIncidentToFix) creates a WorkItem, container and fix drive. The UI path writes an Incident and never routes it, so a hand-opened investigation is a dead end that can only be marked noise. Preserve the human approve then send gate.
items.evidence: lib/incidents.ts — routeIncidentToFix is not referenced anywhere in this module

items.title: Scan completion replaces the blind timer
items.gap: setTimeout(reload, 1500) is unconnected to scan completion — a slower scan reloads into stale state and looks like it did nothing.

items.title: Top bar has zero working controls
items.gap: Command palette and notifications are disabled placeholders. Honest today, but the top bar is prime real estate doing nothing.

items.title: Adopt getAccountState on the last two ad-hoc pages
items.gap: agents/page.tsx and map/page.tsx are the last ad-hoc hasAccess users. The account-state contract says every surface derives from the one resolver.
items.evidence: agents/page.tsx and map/page.tsx contain zero getAccountState references

items.title: A repository at its memory cap can still learn (FIFO archive)
items.works: At the cap the oldest active rows are set to status=archived to make room, inside a serializable transaction — so the old check-then-create race that made the cap advisory is closed too. Read and write caps are the same constant by construction, so they cannot drift.

items.title: Unguarded second checks.update re-opens the double-retry
items.gap: On BOTH worker paths, the checks.update inside the publish-failure catch is itself unguarded. If it throws (transient GitHub failure while reporting a degraded outcome) the error escapes the catch, the job re-throws, and BullMQ retries the whole pipeline despite it having already produced a usable result — the exact double-retry the two-block split was meant to prevent, one call later.
items.evidence: packages/webhook/src/worker.ts — 6 checks.update call sites; the ones inside the publish-failure catch are still unguarded

items.title: Confidence is invisible on pull-request findings
items.works: Confidence is computed and shown on scan and fix work items.
items.gap: ReviewComment has no confidence column at all, so PR findings show none. Schema column plus UI.
items.evidence: packages/db/prisma/schema.prisma — ReviewComment still has no confidence column

items.title: Correct the tracking docs that plans are drawn from
items.gap: backlog.md records 7 entries open that are closed in code, and build-status-map.md §3 A5 / §4 B2, B8 went stale when the retry worker and internal-token work landed. Every plan is drawn off these files, so a stale entry propagates into the next plan.

items.title: Tracing coverage gaps in CI and the webhook
items.gap: Webhook /health is not excluded from tracing (the agents' is), so a 5s probe will dominate span volume. CI smoke-boots dist/otel.js and dist/index.js but never dist/otel-worker.js — the boot path that owns service.name=arete-worker and the whole review span tree is unsmoked.

items.title: Span names and metrics drift from the spec
items.gap: The Python lane emits agent_review:{name} / synthesize_reviews instead of spec §5's agent.review (attr agent.role) / review.synthesize, and no llm.generate span exists. arete.agent.duration has a histogram View registered in both lanes and documented in the skill, but no instrument ever records it. review.publish is wrapped on the PR path and absent on the check_run path. Either rename/record, or amend §5 to match reality — and keep debug-from-telemetry in sync.
items.evidence: orchestrator.py — 8 agent_review:/synthesize_reviews span names still diverge from spec §5

items.title: A locked-out user has no recovery path
items.gap: User.emailVerified exists in the schema and is never written. There is no password reset and no email verification, so a user who loses their password loses their account.
items.evidence: packages/dashboard/src/app — no reset/forgot route; nothing writes User.emailVerified

items.title: The pino log path is unstamped with issue fingerprints
items.gap: otel_exceptions_from_logs_mv and issue_activity_daily read LogAttributes['superlog.issue_fingerprint']; only the span lane is stamped so far, so the log half of those projections cannot group.

items.title: Decide the Python fingerprint question
items.gap: Four record_exception sites in packages/agents need the fingerprint attribute; Python's API accepts attributes=, so the mechanical part is trivial. The blocker is that it needs nine ordered regexes ported — a second implementation, which contract §5 forbids. A drifting copy would split arete-agents errors into two groups with no test able to catch it from either side. Honest options: a shared normalization service, a generated port with golden vectors asserted from both languages, or an explicit spec amendment accepting the copy. A second hand-written copy is forbidden.

items.title: The heavy review lane has a consumer
items.works: startReviewWorkers() starts a separate lower-concurrency Worker for review-pr-heavy alongside the fast lane, and is invoked when worker.ts runs as the entry script. PRs over 50 changed files are no longer routed to a queue nothing drains.

items.title: The send route never advances the work item to posted
items.gap: approve moves fixing to staged, but after a successful send the container becomes posted while its work item stays staged. Not visible locally because STAGING_SERVICE_URL is unset and send honestly 503s.
items.evidence: app/api/containers/[id]/send/route.ts makes no reference to the work item

items.title: Trend charts are off by one day for most timezones
items.gap: lib/trends.ts buckets by LOCAL calendar day while the ClickHouse window is UTC. Every chart in the product is affected for any viewer whose timezone crosses midnight relative to UTC, so the fix needs its own change and its own verification.
items.evidence: packages/dashboard/src/lib/trends.ts — 2 local-calendar date calls against a UTC window

items.title: Docker sandbox workspace agent — run the PR's tests for real
items.gap: The only genuinely missing plan artifact in the repo (workspace.py). Of ~380 symbols declared across 48 plans, this is the one that was never written. It blocks the work-floor Phase B agents.
items.evidence: packages/agents — workspace.py does not exist; still the one missing plan artifact

items.title: Glass Box in production
items.works: The SSE feed, provenance banner and auto-refresh work as a dev sidecar.
items.gap: Structurally undeployable without an installationId filter, and inert in production by design today. Spec is awaiting approval — do not build before it is greenlit.

items.title: Investigations surface + tool-calling fix harness
items.gap: Spec is proposed and awaiting user review — confirm intent before spending a week. Includes giving the fix pipeline a tool loop: fix_pipeline.py makes one direct LLM call for a JSON blob, so spec §3's 'tool descriptions ARE the prompt' cannot apply to healing and rubrics necessarily live in the prompt.
items.evidence: packages/agents/src/arete_agents/fix_pipeline.py — zero tool references, so the fix path still has no tool loop

items.title: Outbound webhook management API + Settings UI
items.works: The tenant-scoped management API is now mounted and authenticated — endpoints can be listed and registered over HTTP, and delivery records exist with signing and retry behind them.
items.gap: What is missing is only the dashboard surface: there is still no screen to register an endpoint or inspect deliveries, so this remains reachable by API alone. The previous claim that the API is "deliberately unmounted" is no longer true.
items.evidence: packages/webhook/src/server.ts:408 — management API still deliberately unmounted

items.title: Tenant telemetry ingestion platform
items.gap: Two-tier ingest edge, ingest keys, strip-then-stamp tenancy, deterministic fingerprint grouping, rollups, background poller and snapshot history, per-tenant retention and a prompt-content consent model. Deliberately deferred — the current stack observes Kuma itself, not the customer's services. Needs a product decision, not code.
items.evidence: No OTLP ingest route or ingest-key handling in packages/webhook/src/server.ts

items.title: Work-floor Phase B agents
items.gap: Repro / root-cause / fix-author / test-author / QA agents. Blocked on the Docker sandbox workspace agent.

items.title: Real account-state signals for every new onboarding step
items.gap: Each new checklist step (workspace connected, install complete, MCP connected) needs a real getAccountState signal before it may show a checkmark. No fabricated ticks. Existing users must land on the correct new step rather than being reset to the start.
items.evidence: lib/overview-setup.ts has 5 coming_soon sub-steps with no backing account-state signal

items.title: Connect Workspace — clone a repo or select a local folder
items.gap: After the GitHub connect step, the user should be able to clone a repo URL OR point at a local folder, so Kuma has the actual working tree it needs for code and telemetry setup. Today this survives only as an honest 'coming soon' sub-step. HARD CONSTRAINT: extend the existing setup — reconcile with GitHub App connect, model connections, and the Sensorium/context-map indexing that already runs on install. Do not greenfield.
items.evidence: lib/overview-setup.ts still carries coming_soon placeholders; no clone or folder-select exists

items.title: Kuma as an MCP server (the inversion)
items.gap: Expose Kuma's findings and review judgment back into the user's coding agent. Only the client half exists today — Kuma can consume third-party MCP servers, but nothing can consume Kuma. This is the 'Connect MCP Kuma to your agent' checklist sub-step.
items.evidence: Only the client half exists (packages/agents/src/arete_agents/mcp/); nothing serves Kuma as an MCP server

items.title: Kuma install — skills, code mapping, telemetry wiring
items.gap: Connecting a coding agent (Claude Code recommended) should kick off the Kuma install into the user's repo: the Kuma skills, the code graph embedded in the project, and OpenTelemetry wired up. The pieces exist separately (agents skills/loader, the context-map checkout and graph export that already run on install) — this is the flow that composes them.
items.evidence: skills/loader.py and context_map/graph_export.py both exist; no flow composes them into an install

items.title: MCP servers are consume-only and CLI-only
items.works: Kuma can consume third-party MCP servers, driven from the CLI.
items.gap: No dashboard surface to add or list them.
items.evidence: no .tsx under components/ or app/ references mcp — still CLI-only

items.title: MCP setup runnable from an agent here, not the IDE
items.gap: The setup should run from an agent inside Kuma rather than requiring the user to do it in their IDE. Depends on the MCP surface being real — today there is no dashboard surface to add or list MCP servers, and the OAuth exchange is simulated.
items.evidence: no dashboard MCP surface exists, so setup still cannot run from an agent here

items.title: The prompt to agent to MCP/skill setup workflow
items.gap: The setup prompt itself — what Kuma hands the user's coding agent to run, and how the result is verified back. Named as a 'coming soon' sub-step on the onboarding checklist with nothing behind it. The user holds reference data on how this is done.
items.evidence: lib/overview-setup.ts — the setup-prompt sub-step is still a coming_soon placeholder

items.title: CI covers orchestration, topology, net-guard and db
items.works: ci.yml runs a package matrix over @arete/orchestration, @arete/topology, @arete/net-guard and @arete/db. The claim that these four had no CI job is no longer true.

items.title: Deploy pipeline, hosted Postgres, production runbook
items.gap: There is no deploy path. Everything runs on a local dogfood server against a shared local Postgres. Needs a pipeline, a hosted database, and a runbook for the three services (dashboard, webhook, agents).
items.evidence: .github/workflows contains only ci.yml — there is no deploy workflow

items.title: The Google OAuth client no longer exists
blockedBy: ext:the Google OAuth client was deleted upstream in Google Cloud Console
items.gap: Deleted upstream in Google Cloud Console. Email/password login is the working path. Either recreate the client or remove the sign-in option rather than leaving a button that cannot succeed.

items.title: Feature commits keep bypassing the integration gate
items.gap: Commits have repeatedly landed directly on a serving branch instead of going lane to integration-preview to a reviewed merge, each time requiring a reverse-merge and suite repair. Duplicate parallel work has also shipped three times (agent-statuses, the healing loop, model switching). A process gap, not a code gap — but it costs real hours.

items.title: Managed environment template
items.gap: Env setup is hand-rolled per worktree. A template that a new environment can be provisioned from, covering OAuth, the internal token, encryption keys and service URLs.

items.title: One command runs every suite
items.works: Root package.json exposes test:all = pnpm run test:ts && pnpm run test:agents.

items.title: Lint ratchets held at warn so CI can be green
items.gap: packages/dashboard holds 51 no-explicit-any at warn; packages/webhook holds 78 no-console at warn and turns no-explicit-any off entirely at the Octokit boundary; packages/agents sets ruff line-length to 120. Each is a deliberate, documented ratchet — flip back to error once paid. Also descoped and never rescheduled: vitest/pytest coverage reporting, advisory only.

items.title: New Anthropic connections default to the slowest model
items.works: Fast tiers exist and the agent role defaults were re-tiered for speed (chat/quality/test_coverage/performance/ci = haiku, security/business_logic/deployment_safety/synthesizer = sonnet).
items.gap: The connect UI still lists claude-opus-4-8 first, so a new connection gets the slowest model by default — and the BYO path runs ALL roles on the one model the user picked, so that choice is not softened by the role tiers.

items.title: Public API & API keys
items.gap: No key issuance, no read or management REST surface.

items.title: Slack / Linear / PagerDuty relays
items.gap: Each relay is a thin consumer once outbound webhook management lands. The retry worker is already running, so this is the last dependency.
items.evidence: No implementation exists: the only Slack/Linear/PagerDuty mentions in packages/webhook/src and packages/dashboard/src are comments in outbound/management.ts:10 and outbound/payload.ts:4 describing the future feature

items.title: Review.prUrl + resolve / noise reason codes
items.gap: A review does not store a link back to its pull request, and resolving or silencing a finding records no reason.
items.evidence: packages/db/prisma/schema.prisma — Review has no prUrl column

items.title: Review scope filters
items.gap: No way to say which paths Kuma should or should not review. Every PR is reviewed whole.
items.evidence: No review-scope or path-filter implementation exists

items.title: No self-serve upgrade
items.works: Real billing usage is shown against the free-tier limit, and the absence of an upgrade path is stated plainly rather than faked.
items.gap: A user who hits the limit cannot pay. Stripe groundwork exists in packages/webhook.

items.title: Telemetry snapshots go stale with no indication
items.works: The grid reads a real telemetry snapshot per repository.
items.gap: Snapshots are captured only during a review and there is no background poller, so a quiet repo shows arbitrarily stale data with nothing but fetchedAt to say so. TelemetrySnapshotRecord is a latest-value row, not a history table.
items.evidence: packages/webhook/src/telemetry — no setInterval or cron; snapshots are captured only during a review

items.title: OpenTelemetry is 95% dark to the UI
items.works: Full OpenTelemetry instrumentation flows into ClickHouse and Jaeger, and Jaeger trace deep-links exist.
items.gap: Still no in-product trace view, no span drill-down and no log search. One correction: deep-linking is no longer missing — the Errors list and an incident's Signals rows turn real trace ids into Jaeger links when NEXT_PUBLIC_JAEGER_UI_URL is set, and render plain text when it is not.

items.title: The Anthropic account is at $0
blockedBy: ext:the Anthropic account is at $0 - add credits or run on Ollama
items.gap: Not a code problem and not fixable from here. Agent work runs on local Ollama until credits are added. Several other parked items are gated on this, because they need a funded key to measure. A lost provider key must be regenerated — providers do not re-issue them.

items.title: ClickHouse TTLs are configured but unverified live
blockedBy: ext:needs real aged data - every row here is hours old
items.gap: 30d raw / 90d rollup with ttl_only_drop_parts=1 is correct on paper, but every row in this ClickHouse is hours old — nowhere near the retention window. Needs real aged data to confirm parts actually drop.

items.title: Browser QA on the code-map reading panel
blockedBy: ext:a human browser pass a unit suite cannot stand in for
items.gap: A real browser pass that a unit suite cannot stand in for. A human task, not an agent one.

items.title: End-to-end ERROR-severity log path is unexercised
blockedBy: ext:needs an ERROR-severity row to exist; the dashboard exports traces only
items.gap: otel_logs has zero rows at ERROR+ because the dashboard exports traces only. Blocked on such a row existing, not on code — the SQL, timestamp format and column projection are already verified against real rows at a lowered threshold.

items.title: Is haiku adequate to AUTHOR fixes?
blockedBy: ext:needs a funded provider key plus a regression corpus to measure
items.gap: Fix authoring reuses each dimension's configured REVIEW tier, and performance/quality/test_coverage default to haiku. Haiku's original job is critiquing a diff, not generating a complete file replacement. Do NOT change the tier blind: needs a funded key plus a regression corpus of known-fixable issues to measure haiku-vs-sonnet patch pass rate first.

items.title: MCP RFC 8414 discovery + dynamic client registration
blockedBy: ext:speculative until a real MCP server with no static token_url needs it
items.gap: Speculative until a real MCP server that hands out no static token_url needs it, and nothing in this environment validates such a flow.

items.title: ReviewComment index choices
blockedBy: ext:needs EXPLAIN ANALYZE at scale before any index is added
items.gap: May still be missing indexes on severity, category and noiseState. The reviewId index cut 25-66% off the four hottest queries at synthetic scale, but that number entangles the join index with these per-column filters. Measure each individually with EXPLAIN ANALYZE before adding any — do not guess.

items.title: Review LLM fan-out is unbounded per review
blockedBy: ext:needs a real large PR and a funded key to measure
items.gap: LangGraph fans out one node per (file x agent) pair. A 20-file PR reviewed by 6 agents is ~120 concurrent LLM calls for ONE review; five concurrent reviews is up to ~600 simultaneous provider calls. The real throughput bottleneck is likely the provider's rate limit, not REVIEW_QUEUE_CONCURRENCY=5. The max_concurrency default is also untuned. Both need a real large PR and a funded key to measure.

items.title: Review job retries duplicate the per-agent retry
blockedBy: ext:needs a real induced transient provider failure mid-review
items.gap: processGitHubPullRequest re-throws any pipeline failure and BullMQ's attempts:3 then re-runs the ENTIRE multi-file multi-agent review from scratch, on top of each agent's own retry. Needs a real induced transient provider failure mid-review to count the actual duplicated calls. Likely fix once measured: distinguish 'some agents failed, partial result produced' from 'the pipeline crashed for an infra reason'.

items.title: Make Signals visibly render
blockedBy: ext:needs a real Alertmanager alert - synthetic seeds are forbidden
items.gap: Needs a real Alertmanager alert. Synthetic seeds are forbidden — seeding one would be fabricated data on the exact surface whose job is to be trustworthy.
